title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the distance the football travels, we need to consider the horizontal component of the velocity and the time it is in the air.

title: Horizontal Velocity Component
text: The horizontal component of the velocity can be found using the formula: \(v_x=v*cos(theta)\), where \(v\) is the initial velocity and \(theta\) is the angle of projection.

title: Time of Flight
text: The time the football is in the air can be found using the vertical component of the velocity and the formula: \(t=2*v*sin(theta)/g\), where \(g\) is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Calculate Horizontal Distance
text: Using the horizontal velocity and time of flight, calculate the horizontal distance using the formula: \(d=v_x*t\).
